seoTitle: How to Remove the Made in Webflow Badge
metaDescription: Hide the Made in Webflow badge and platform HTML references from your site settings for a fully white-label result.
intro: Presenting a clean, unbranded site matters for freelancers and agencies handing off finished projects to clients. This guide covers turning off the visible Webflow badge and the underlying branding references in your site's HTML through the Site Settings panel. It's for anyone who wants a fully white-labeled delivery.

steps.heading: Open Site Settings
steps.body: From the top of the Designer, drop out of the canvas view and open the Site Settings console.

steps.heading: Find the branding section
steps.body: Navigate to the General tab and locate the section covering platform branding options.

steps.heading: Disable the visual badge
steps.body: Turn off the Display Made in Webflow Badge toggle to remove the visible platform mark from your live site.

steps.heading: Disable HTML branding references
steps.body: Also switch off Display Webflow Branding in HTML Code so your underlying markup stays clean of platform references.

steps.heading: Save and republish
steps.body: Save your settings changes, then publish the site to your custom domain and check the live version to confirm the badge is gone.

faq.question: Do I need a paid plan to remove the badge?
faq.answer: Yes, toggling off the Webflow badge typically requires a paid Site Plan or a specific Workspace tier like Growth or Agency.

faq.question: Will removing the badge affect my site's functionality?
faq.answer: No, it's purely a visual and markup change and won't impact how your site works.

faq.question: Do I need to do this on every page individually?
faq.answer: No, this is a global site setting that applies across your entire project once toggled off.

faq.question: Why is the badge still showing after I turned it off?
faq.answer: Make sure you republished the site after saving the setting, since changes only apply to live pages after a fresh publish.
